title: Infineon wins patent infringement case against Innoscience
author: Prnewswire
description: Infineon Technologies won two patent infringement cases against Chinese company Innoscience at the District Court Munich regarding unauthorized use of GaN (gallium nitride) technology. The court prohibited Innoscience from manufacturing and selling infringing products in Germany and ordered damages payment. This marks Innoscience's third and fourth legal defeat, with additional proceedings pending in the U.S. and Germany.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Infineon won multiple patent infringement cases against a competitor, demonstrating the strength of its intellectual property portfolio and ability to defend its GaN technology. The court rulings validate Infineon's technology leadership and provide legal protection for its market position in the growing GaN semiconductor market.

title: S&P 500 Selloff Looks More Like Rotation Than Market Breakdown
author: Itai Smidt
description: A stronger-than-expected May jobs report (172,000 payrolls vs. 80,000-105,000 forecast) triggered a market rotation rather than a broad selloff. The strong labor data pushed Treasury yields to 4.54%, reducing rate-cut expectations and hitting rate-sensitive tech stocks. While the Nasdaq fell 1.13% and semiconductor stocks cratered, the Russell 2000 surged 1.45% as money rotated into cyclicals and small caps. The Dow held near record highs, indicating broadening market participation rather than a systemic breakdown.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock tumbled 12% Thursday after leaving full-year AI chip targets unchanged, which markets interpreted as a miss. Continued selling Friday with additional 3% decline as the unwind fed on itself, triggering broader semiconductor selloff.

Down approximately 1% on the day, showing relative strength compared to peers but still declining. Despite weakness, held up better than other chip stocks, suggesting leadership hasn't broken but is exhaling.

title: U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) rules in favor of Infineon and orders import and sales bans against Innoscience
author: Prnewswire
description: The U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) ruled in favor of Infineon Technologies in a patent infringement case against Innoscience, ordering import and sales bans for gallium nitride (GaN) products in the US. The decision affirms an initial determination from December 2025 and is subject to a 60-day presidential review period. Infineon, a leader in GaN technology with approximately 450 GaN patent families, is also pursuing parallel infringement cases against Innoscience in German courts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Infineon won a significant patent infringement case with import and sales bans ordered against a competitor. This protects their market position, validates their extensive GaN patent portfolio, and reinforces their competitive advantage in the high-growth GaN semiconductor market used in renewable energy, AI data centers, and electric vehicles.

title: Germany's Merz Backs US Alliance, Calls Iran Nuclear Threat 'Common Goal' As Trump Pulls Troops Over War Rift
author: Namrata Sen
description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz affirmed Germany's commitment to the US-NATO alliance while acknowledging differences with Trump over Iran strategy. Trump announced withdrawal of 5,000+ troops from Germany and imposed 25% tariffs on EU vehicles, impacting German automakers and tech firms.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: German automaker faces 25% tariffs on EU cars and trucks announced by Trump, directly impacting export competitiveness and profitability.
German tech firm likely to face pressure from broader trade tensions and tariffs resulting from US-EU trade disputes.
German semiconductor company exposed to trade tensions and potential tariffs affecting EU tech exports to the US market.
German automaker subject to 25% tariffs on EU vehicles, creating significant headwinds for sales and margins in the US market.

title: Trump Says EU 'Not Complying' With Trade Deal, Announces 25% Tariffs On Cars, Trucks
author: Snigdha Gairola
description: President Trump announced a 25% tariff on EU cars and trucks, claiming the European Union is not complying with a previously agreed trade deal. The tariffs will take effect next week, though vehicles produced in U.S. plants will be exempt. Trump cited over $100 billion in domestic auto plant investments. The EU is considering retaliatory measures, and analysts warn that exporters relying on the U.S. market could face higher costs and reduced competitiveness.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: European tech company with U.S. exposure that could face pressure from escalating trade tensions and potential retaliatory measures in the ongoing trade dispute.
European semiconductor company with U.S. market exposure that faces potential pressure from trade tensions and possible EU retaliatory measures.
Dutch tech company with significant U.S. sales exposure that could be negatively impacted by escalating trade tensions and potential retaliatory measures.

As a major European automaker with significant U.S. sales exposure, Mercedes-Benz faces direct impact from the 25% tariff on imported vehicles, which will increase costs and reduce competitiveness in the U.S. market.
As a European luxury goods company with significant U.S. market exposure, LVMH faces potential pressure from broader trade tensions and possible retaliatory measures by the EU.
